compiled_state.title: Tier C: port validate (validate-issues.sh ~945 LOC) to Python behind REBAR_COMPUTE
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Port validate (validate-issues.sh ~945 LOC, 10 python3 heredocs + 11 check_* bash functions) to Python behind REBAR_COMPUTE, the second/last real port of Tier C (next-batch done in sure-beech-taunt). validate is repo-wide tracker health (takes NO ticket id), exit code score-encoded (exit == 5 - score). Unlike next-batch it is genuinely bash (per-check shell functions assembling finding buckets), so a heavier literal translation reusing rebar.reducer/rebar.graph.

## Scope
- New module(s) under rebar._engine_support (e.g. validate.py) reusing reduce_all_tickets/build_dep_graph; route ticket-reads.py validate + dispatcher arm via _compute_python (helper already added); library rebar.validate() + MCP in-process under the switch.
- Parity surface = finding bucketing + severity/ordering determinism (critical/major/minor/warning order, per-check emission order) and the score calculation + exit-code mapping. Pin with a byte-exact golden matrix (sorted ordering; no bash sort/set nondeterminism).
- Keep test files <1000 LOC; obey module-size policy (split heredoc clusters on real seams).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] validate ported to rebar._engine_support, reusing reducer/graph (no re-implemented reducer)
- [ ] dispatcher + ticket-reads.py + library/MCP routed via REBAR_COMPUTE; default bash until flip+soak
- [ ] dual-run byte-identical (stdout/stderr + score-encoded exit) across bash|python for the golden matrix
- [ ] each check_* finding bucket + severity order pinned deterministically
- [ ] bash suite test-ticket-validate.sh passes under both switch values; new pytest gate added
- [ ] default stays bash pending flip+soak; validate-issues.sh retires with the tier

comments.body:
Ported validate behind REBAR_COMPUTE (Tier C).

Impl: rebar._engine_support.validate (normalize + score + render + CLI, 330 LOC) + validate_checks (the 9 check_* functions, 281 LOC, each returning an ordered Finding stream so severity buckets reproduce byte-for-byte). Data source mirrors bash: subprocess $TICKET_CMD list when injected (the bash suite's test mechanism), else in-process list_states (production). Self-manages freshness (reads.main skips its global prefetch via _NO_PREFETCH).

Switch-in: same dispatcher _compute_python helper as next-batch; new validate arm + ticket-reads.py validate handler; library rebar.validate() + MCP in-process under the switch.

Parity (docs/bash-migration.md §1.4): text/terse/verbose stderr + score-encoded exit byte-identical across REBAR_COMPUTE=bash|python (ANSI colors + the bash 'shared cache' re-fetch verbose artifact reproduced); --output json pinned by schema + semantic equality (jq vs json.dumps whitespace is out of contract). bash suite test-ticket-validate.sh 10/10 under both switch values. New gate tests/interfaces/test_validate_compute.py (20 cases). Real-store in-process parity verified (score 1 on this repo, both impls agree). Interfaces tier 288/288.

Status: default stays bash pending flip+soak; validate-issues.sh retires with the tier.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
